verses.text: David disclaims a haughty heart and lofty eyes, not meddling in 'great matters... too high for me.' He has learned humility. The contented soul does not strive after things beyond its place but rests in God's appointment.

He has 'behaved and quieted myself, as a child that is weaned of his mother,' his soul calm within him. He no longer frets for what he cannot have. A weaned heart finds peace in God rather than in its old cravings.
He turns to urge, 'Let Israel hope in the LORD from henceforth and for ever.' His own calm becomes a call to others. The hope that stills one heart is the hope for all God's people.
